summary: IllegalArgumentException when using ".filter" on observable produced by RxObservables.create
description:
At line 85 in RxObservables.java there is a call to set the producer on the Rx subscriber. When this subscriber is from OperatorFilter it will call the request method with a value of 0.  At line 92 in RxObservables this is forwarded to the mongo subscriber and triggers an IllegalArgumentException in com.mongodb.async.client.AbstractSubscription.

Given that 0 is a legal request value in the Rx world it seems that either the mongo code will need to adjust or we need to avoid making the forwarded call to request when the value is 0.

To reproduce simply take the observable provided by MongoCollection.find() and add call .filter on it prior to subscribing.

summary: Upgrade RxJava and Mongo-async-driver dependencies
Deprecate methods related to inline results for the aggregate command
Update to Mongo Java Driver Async 3.5
Update RxJava to 1.3.0
Deprecate modifiers in FindObservable and replace with properties
Typos in Mechanism names
description:
Bumped into what look to be a couple typos on HEAD. Missing the "DB" in "MONGODB-CR", "MONGODB-X509" here:

http://bazaar.launchpad.net/+branch/mgo/v2/view/head:/auth.go#L179
http://bazaar.launchpad.net/+branch/mgo/v2/view/head:/auth.go#L181

Use case: client code was being explicit about specifying Mechanism: "MONGODB-CR", which falls into the else path to try SASL instead of "classic" auth.

(Of course a simple workaround's available.)

summary: 'Indexes' should use listIndexes command
description:
Currently, the 'Indexes' method queries for all entries in 'system.indexes'.

However, in 3.0WT, we no longer have the 'system.indexes' collection. Instead, the method should use the listIndexes command.

summary: EnsureIndex should use createIndex command
description:
Currently, the 'EnsureIndex' method creates indexes by calling 'Insert' into "system.indexes". 

However, in 3.0WT, we no longer have the 'system.indexes' collection. Instead, the method should use the createIndex command.

summary: Timeout to DialWithInfo not used as expected
description:
If I follow correctly, the {{Timeout}} value specified on a {{DialInfo}} is not considered by either the initial cluster sync or {{pinger}} before the {{Session}} is returned to the user.

So for example, a fail-fast dial with a timeout of 3 seconds will not actually timeout until 20 seconds. (Two connection attempts that first waits for 15s and then for 5s.)

Simple reproducer program attached that attempts to dial a firewalled machine, which will timeout.

summary: Support greedy authentication
Support cursor in listCollections and listIndexes commands
cursor may lost data
description:
we lost oplog's data when tail the oplog use mgo cusor.
In normal env, the cursor will work very well, but in packet-loss network env, the mgo cursor maybe lost data.

socket.go:
func (socket *mongoSocket) readLoop()

This function read document one by one for OP_REPLY:
1. if partial packet is lost for THIS reply
2. readLoop will be wait for lost data
3. cursor send another GetMore
4. data corrupted

summary: bson.D should support JSON serialization
document interaction between Timeout and DialServer in DialInfo
description: It is not obvious from the documentation of {{DialInfo}} that {{Timeout}} does not apply if you pass in a {{DialServer}}.

summary: Change nUpdated to nMatched in bulk api results
GridFS completeWrite doesn't create unique index
description:
According to the [documentation|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/core/gridfs/#gridfs-index], a unique, compound index on the chunks collection - for the files_id and n fields - should be created when storing files.

However, we don't create a _unique_ index on these fields (see [here|https://github.com/go-mgo/mgo/blob/9e7209e40e2f40d55688f225f9204a6e8eb93192/gridfs.go#L531]) and don't verify that its creation succeeded.

summary: v2 does not compile with go 1.6
description:
The v2 branch does not compile with Go 1.6. You get the following error from running "go get" : 
{code}
-- Failed to get github.com/go-mgo/mgo, error:
package github.com/go-mgo/mgo
	imports gopkg.in/mgo.v2-unstable/internal/scram: use of internal package not allowed
{code}

This blocks the Automation Agent from upgrading to Go 1.6.

summary: a read preference of mgo.Secondary doesn't allow connections to a mongos
description:
Here is a reproduction case. When I run this program against a sharded replica set, the second query fails with a "no reachable servers"

{noformat}
package main

import (
        "fmt"
        "log"
        "os"
        "time"

        "gopkg.in/mgo.v2"
        "gopkg.in/mgo.v2/bson"
)

func main() {
        mgo.SetLogger(log.New(os.Stderr, "MGO: ", 0))
        mgo.SetDebug(false)
        session, err := mgo.Dial("localhost:27017")
        if err != nil {
                fmt.Print("Dial: %v", err)
                os.Exit(1)
        }
        session.SetSocketTimeout(4 * time.Second)
        session.SetSyncTimeout(4 * time.Second)
        result := &bson.M{}

        session.SetMode(mgo.Primary, true)
        err = session.DB("test").C("test").Find(nil).One(result)
        fmt.Printf("mode = primary  err: %v, result: %v\n", err, result)

        session.SetMode(mgo.Secondary, true)
        err = session.DB("test").C("test").Find(nil).One(result)
        fmt.Printf("mode = secondary err: %v, result: %v\n", err, result)
}
{noformat}

I assume the trouble arises from https://github.com/go-mgo/mgo/blob/v2-unstable/cluster.go#L591 where the !(slaveOk && mode == Secondary) clause should not apply when connecting to a mongos.

Maybe there should be a isMongo bool on the cluster. I thought about trying to implement this, but it wasn't clear weather it was ok to assume that clusters will be homogeneously all mongos or all not mongos, and what one would have to do if the cluster was heterogeneous.

summary: Support GridFS custom file_id methods
description:
The updated [GridFS Spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst] adds two new methods:
* open_upload_stream_with_id
* upload_from_stream_with_id

Both need to be implemented and tested.

summary: BuildInfo.VersionAtLeast not considering value significance
Additional unmarshal to bson.M performed for commands
Add support for Decimal128 clamping and rounding
description:
See MGO-107.  

This issue is to track merging of Decimal128 clamping and rounding support once it hits v2-unstable.

summary: OP_GET_MORE is used when iterating aggregation cursors
description: The mgo driver uses OP_GET_MORE to iterate aggregation cursors.  This works fine in 3.2, but fails in 3.4 when aggregating a read-only view.  Read-only views are not supported by OP_GET_MORE and require use of the getMore command.

summary: Performance degradation using the find and getMore commands
description:
mgo's performance degrades severely with use of the find and getMore commands, as compared with OP_QUERY and OP_GET_MORE.  The issue has been traced to the lowest levels of mgo's bson decoder, which is inefficient in the way it decodes a BSON array into a []bson.Raw.  Rather than simply skipping over the content of each document, it instead iterates all the keys and values and discards them.

A fix has been identified, though it will result in a behavior change: corruption of a document that is decoded into bson.Raw will only be detected upon unmarshalling of the bson.Raw's bytes, rather than during unmarshalling into the bson.Raw itself.

summary: Implement bson-corpus specification tests
description:
See https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/bson-corpus


The assertions related to the extended JSON specification are not in scope.

summary: JSON parsing bug for quoted keys
Prevent panic when Collection.NewIter called with error set
description: Collection.NewIter will panic when an error is set and the sockets are also nil. David pointed out this can be avoided by returning early and treating the iter as one with an error (as it should be).

summary: Prefer channel communication to stop pinger goroutine early
description: The pinger (https://github.com/10gen/mgo/blob/v2-unstable/server.go#L301) always sleeps for 15 seconds and during this time the server can be closed. This lets us stop the goroutine early. You can imagine without connection pooling done by the user, creating many new mgo clients can cause the number of sleeping goroutines to rise very quickly.

summary: Update SCRAM-SHA-256 to not SASLprep usernames
Deadlock on Iter.Next double mutex acquisition
description:
While iterating on a tailable cursor in the backup agent, we've encountered at least 1 occurrence where the call to {{Iter.Next()}} hangs forever in a deadlock. The cause appears to be the caller's goroutine attempts to lock the {{Iter}}'s mutex a second time. 

Relevant goroutine hang:
{noformat}
goroutine 1429175 [semacquire, 434 minutes]:
sync.runtime_Semacquire(0xc4202c22d4)
        /opt/go/src/runtime/sema.go:47 +0x30
sync.(*Mutex).Lock(0xc4202c22d0)
        /opt/go/src/sync/mutex.go:85 +0xd0
gopkg.in/mgo%2ev2.(*Iter).replyFunc.func1(0xc92a00, 0xc42000c150, 0x0, 0xffffffffffffffff, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /data/mci/4b33f4d9a17c2015f24d5e00504426c5/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/gopkg.in/mgo.v2/session.go:4584 +0x64
gopkg.in/mgo%2ev2.(*mongoSocket).Query(0xc4203bb880, 0xc42014db88, 0x1, 0x1, 0x1, 0xc42014db88)
        /data/mci/4b33f4d9a17c2015f24d5e00504426c5/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/gopkg.in/mgo.v2/socket.go:501 +0x2f51
gopkg.in/mgo%2ev2.(*Iter).getMore(0xc4202c22d0)
        /data/mci/4b33f4d9a17c2015f24d5e00504426c5/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/gopkg.in/mgo.v2/session.go:4001 +0x1f8
gopkg.in/mgo%2ev2.(*Iter).Next(0xc4202c22d0, 0x81a7a0, 0xc4203a0ce0, 0xc4203c0b00)
        /data/mci/4b33f4d9a17c2015f24d5e00504426c5/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/gopkg.in/mgo.v2/session.go:3784 +0x102
10gen.com/backup-agent/components.(*Tail).TailOplog(0xc4201c4210, 0xc4203c0be0, 0xc4203753e0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /data/mci/4b33f4d9a17c2015f24d5e00504426c5/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/10gen.com/backup-agent/components/oplog.go:267 +0x779
created by 10gen.com/backup-agent/components.(*Agent).manageTail
        /data/mci/4b33f4d9a17c2015f24d5e00504426c5/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/10gen.com/backup-agent/components/agent.go:365 +0x2f2
{noformat}

From the path, we know that we got an error from the socket and mgo marked it as {{dead}}, sending us down this path:

https://github.com/10gen/mgo/blob/97c81e4d2a064c4ed28dd5e1226e711605c4af76/socket.go?utf8=%E2%9C%93#L501

The deadlock comes from the same goroutine attempting to lock the {{Iter}}'s mutex while it's already holding the lock.

First acquire on {{iter.Next()}} from oplog tail:
https://github.com/10gen/mgo/blob/97c81e4d2a064c4ed28dd5e1226e711605c4af76/session.go#L3980

Second acquire attempt that hangs forever:
https://github.com/10gen/mgo/blob/97c81e4d2a064c4ed28dd5e1226e711605c4af76/session.go?utf8=%E2%9C%93#L4584

summary: Deadlock on Iter.Next from never receiving gotReply broadcast
description:
Whoops, I think an unfortunate side-effect of MGO-156 is it's possible for {{Wait()}}/{{Broadcast()}} to be out of sync. 

We've encountered a similar deadlock as MGO-156 in the backup agent, however with a different backtrace -- now we're hung never receiving the broadcast to {{iter.gotReply}}.

{noformat}
goroutine 235 [semacquire, 135 minutes]:
sync.runtime_notifyListWait(0xc4200c06a8, 0xc40016e806)
        /opt/go/src/runtime/sema.go:267 +0x122
sync.(*Cond).Wait(0xc4200c0698)
        /opt/go/src/sync/cond.go:57 +0x80
gopkg.in/mgo%2ev2.(*Iter).Next(0xc4200c0690, 0x86e040, 0xc4202622e0, 0xc4202622c0)
        /data/mci/6fead07a0c247c6943e899496bd8fdd3/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/gopkg.in/mgo.v2/session.go:3854 +0x135
10gen.com/backup-agent/components.(*Tail).TailOplog(0xc4201ba420, 0xc4202622c0, 0xc420323b60, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /data/mci/6fead07a0c247c6943e899496bd8fdd3/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/10gen.com/backup-agent/components/oplog.go:267 +0x779
created by 10gen.com/backup-agent/components.(*Agent).manageTail
        /data/mci/6fead07a0c247c6943e899496bd8fdd3/mms-backup/build/build/go-dependencies/src/10gen.com/backup-agent/components/agent.go:365 +0x2f2
{noformat}

I wonder if perhaps due to possible calling of Wait and Broadcast from same goroutine -- but not certain about this. E.g.,

{code:golang}
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"sync"
)

func main() {
	c := sync.Cond{L: &sync.Mutex{}}
	
	c.L.Lock()
	fmt.Println("Broadcast", c)
	c.Broadcast()
	c.L.Unlock()

	c.L.Lock()
	fmt.Println("Wait", c)
	c.Wait()	
	c.L.Unlock()
}
{code}

Output: https://play.golang.org/p/B1twpmCBm7H
{noformat}
Broadcast {{} 0x10414020 {0 0 0 <nil> <nil>} 0}
Wait {{} 0x10414020 {0 0 0 <nil> <nil>} 272925276}
fatal error: all goroutines are asleep - deadlock!

goroutine 1 [semacquire]:
sync.runtime_notifyListWait(0x10448248, 0x0)
	/usr/local/go/src/runtime/sema.go:510 +0x140
sync.(*Cond).Wait(0x10448240, 0x2)
	/usr/local/go/src/sync/cond.go:56 +0xc0
main.main()
	/tmp/sandbox457902971/main.go:19 +0x2e0
{noformat}

summary: Support marshaling/unmarshaling to mongo-go-driver/bson
description: Based on [~eric.daniels] work: https://github.com/10gen/mgo/pull/19

summary: Tailable cursors sometimes timeout later than they should
description:
Tailable cursors are supposed to short-circuit a call to Next() after the specified timeout. However, if a getMore is already running in the background when Next is called, then Next may wait for that getMore to finish before calculating the deadline. This means that Next will wait longer than expected, possibly waiting for a *second getMore* even after the timeout has expired.

This bug was caused by https://github.com/go-mgo/mgo/commit/fc6ec8bbb79b603af9d073196129bd278a46f540

The fix is to calculate the deadline regardless of the value for {{iter.docsToReceive}}.

summary: NoCursorTimeout is not respected for findCmd
description: Looks like [findCmd.NoCursorTimeout|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/blob/7b16532d4653bcfcb5b3c919c7610cca8035af04/vendor/src/gopkg.in/mgo.v2/session.go#L3344-L3369] [is not explicitly set|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/blob/7b16532d4653bcfcb5b3c919c7610cca8035af04/vendor/src/gopkg.in/mgo.v2/session.go#L3293-L3307] among maybe a few other fields.

summary: mgo driver causing high DNS requests
description:
This is related to SF-00377004.

The customer was unhappy with a high number of DNS requests being generated. It was found that a number of down/unhealthy hosts, as well as the aggressive mgo driver retry attempts to be the root cause of their issue. 

100ms is the hardcoded period that mgo uses in certain connection failure cases, such as to retry to find the primary for example: 
https://github.com/go-mgo/mgo/blob/f4923a569136442e900b8cf5c1a706c0a8b0883c/cluster.go#L597

Is it possible to have the mgo driver retry time to increased from 100ms (or made user configurable)? The customer has requested that this be implemented. I'm not to sure whether this would be a high priority as this "high" request rate is not likely to be an issue in most cases. Citigroup appear to have a large number of hosts, with many in unhealthy condition. Though, they are aware of this and are removing the hosts with issues (which is a workaround to their high DNS request issue).

summary: panic listing indexes if keys are not floats
description: If indexes are created using "NumberLong(1)" instead of just "1", mgo will panic enumerating indexes.

summary: Update PyMongo dependency from 2.7.1 to 2.8
get_socket() can time out immediately if waitQueueMultiple is set
description: MotorPool doesn't clear a prior timeout, so if a get_socket() timeout is followed by a series of get_socket() operations, some of those operations may time out immediately instead of waiting the allotted period.

summary: Replica set client fails first reconnect after network error in auth
description: After a call to authenticate(), MotorReplicaSetClient caches the new credentials. If it hits a network error authenticating a different socket with the cached credentials, it should throw AutoReconnect, then reconnect on the first following operation. Instead, the next operation *also* throws AutoReconnect, and only the operation after that succeeds.

summary: Pip pymongo and motor version mismatch
description: Motor requires pymongo exactly at 2.7.1, but pip install installs 2.7.2

summary: Motor Deadlocks when Running Parallel Requests
description:
I've come across an issue where Motor will deadlock under heavy load or when running parallel queries.

If I put my query in a module-level function like:
{code:python}
@gen.coroutine
def get_data():
    cur1 = _client.test.testcol1.find()
    cur2 = _client.test.testcol2.find()
        
    data1, data2 = yield [cur1.to_list(length=None), cur2.to_list(length=None)]
    
    raise gen.Return((data1, data2))
{code}

And then call that function from a RequestHandler like:
{code:python}
class MainHandler(web.RequestHandler):
    @gen.coroutine
    def get(self):
        yield get_data()
        
        self.write("done")
{code}

Motor will always deadlock at:
{noformat}
# ThreadID: 140520054396672
File: "/home/bmiller/opt/python/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/motor/__init__.py", line 683, in call_method
  result = sync_method(self.delegate, *args, **kwargs)
File: "/home/bmiller/opt/python/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pymongo/cursor.py", line 1020, in _refresh
  self.__uuid_subtype))
File: "/home/bmiller/opt/python/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pymongo/cursor.py", line 933, in __send_message
  res = client._send_message_with_response(message, **kwargs)
File: "/home/bmiller/opt/python/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pymongo/mongo_replica_set_client.py", line 1597, in _send_message_with_response
  self._ensure_connected()
File: "/home/bmiller/opt/python/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pymongo/mongo_replica_set_client.py", line 1318, in _ensure_connected
  self.__ensure_monitor()
File: "/home/bmiller/opt/python/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pymongo/mongo_replica_set_client.py", line 1087, in __ensure_monitor
  self.__monitor_lock.acquire()
{noformat}

However, if I put the code directly inside the get method of my RequestHandler it will work fine.

Here's a simple Tornado app that I threw together that will reproduce the issue:
https://gist.github.com/brentalanmiller/efb184b495cdb92d4524

Thanks,
Brent

summary: Docs style
description: As with PyMongo, restyle Motor's sphinx-generated documentation with the Python 3 standard stylesheet.

summary: Cursor.close() from cursor-each callback doesn't stop iteration
description:
As demonstrated in TestAsyncIOCursor.test_each_close. 
{code}
    @asyncio_test
    def test_each_close(self):
        loop = self.loop
        collection = self.collection
        results = []
        future = asyncio.Future(loop=self.loop)

        def callback(result, error):
            results.append(result)
            if len(results) == 50:
                # Prevent further calls.
                asyncio.Task(cursor.close(), loop=self.loop)
                loop.call_later(0.1, partial(future.set_result, None))

        cursor = collection.find()
        cursor.each(callback)
        yield from future
        self.assertGreater(150, len(results))
{code}
"cursor.close" is supposed to set "cursor.closed" to True and stop iteration promptly - this should be the last call to "callback". "cursor.close" is a coroutine, but in Tornado this works anyway, since calling a coroutine without "yield" starts the coroutine and runs it to its first yield.

In asyncio, however, calling a coroutine without "yield from" does nothing. (Therefore, Nikolay Novik wrapped it in asyncio.Task when he ported test_each_close from Motor's tests to asyncio. But, since the Task runs asynchronously at an unpredictable future event loop iteration, it does not reliably cancel iteration with "each".) The "each" callback should "yield from cursor.close()" to run the coroutine, but the "each" callback isn't allowed to be a coroutine.

This doesn't really have to work, anyway: the "each" callback has a reliable method of canceling iteration, which is to return False. That's the documented method for canceling reliably. So probably close this wontfix, remove the tests, and check that the documentation is consistent on this point: the only right way to cancel iteration from within the callback is to return False.

summary: Replace unreliable cursor-close tests with MockupDB
description:
How Motor currently tests that it sends killCursors correctly is very hacky: it copies the closing cursor's id and nRetrieved into a new cursor and periodically attempts a getMore on the copied cursor until it gets a characteristic server message that shows the cursor is gone on the server.

Instead, depend on [MockupDB|http://mockupdb.readthedocs.org/] and use it to test directly that a killCursors message is received.

summary: socket_keep_alive for asyncio
description: In Andrew Svetlov's version of Motor with asyncio support, test_socketKeepAlive shows that the socketKeepAlive option to AsyncIOMotorClient doesn't work. Fix that and re-verify the Tornado MotorClient support for socketKeepAlive.

summary: Forward-port MOTOR-58
Drop support for indexing Motor cursors with "[n]"
Incorrect iteration of MotorCursor causes infinite loop
